name: Bahala Na (Nalang) Gang
description: The Bahala Na Gang (BNG) is a Filipino street gang founded in 1940 in Sampaloc, Manila, Philippines, by Divino Talastas. The name means 'come what may' in Filipino. It spread to Filipino immigrant communities in California — San Diego, Los Angeles, San Jose, San Francisco, Stockton, Sacramento — and Las Vegas. Members are involved in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, murder, robbery, and kidnapping. In 1990, a gang fight at a Stockton taco stand involving a dozen BNG members killed two and injured five, resulting in 348 criminal counts.
